Pandowdy Apple IIe Emulator
Table of Contents

Class Slots

Namespace
Pandowdy.EmuCore
Assembly
Pandowdy.EmuCore.dll

Implements the Apple IIe expansion slot system, managing peripheral cards and their I/O and ROM access.

public class Slots : ISlots, IMemory, IConfigurable
Inheritance
object
Slots
Implements
ISlots
IMemory
IConfigurable
Inherited Members
object.Equals(object)
object.Equals(object, object)
object.GetHashCode()
object.GetType()
object.MemberwiseClone()
object.ReferenceEquals(object, object)
object.ToString()

Remarks

The Slots class is the concrete implementation of ISlots, providing the complete address decoding logic for the Apple IIe's $C000-$CFFF peripheral I/O range. It coordinates between peripheral cards, system ROM, and soft switch settings to determine which device responds to memory accesses.

Architecture:
The slots system maintains an array of 8 card positions (0-7), where slot 0 is reserved for system use and slots 1-7 are available for peripheral cards. Empty slots are filled with NullCard instances that return null for all operations, causing floating bus behavior.

Address Decoding Priority:

  1. INTCXROM check - If enabled, system ROM overrides all cards
  2. Address range decode - Determines I/O, ROM, or Extended ROM space
  3. SLOTCXROM/SLOTC3ROM check - Determines card vs. system ROM (when INTCXROM off)
  4. Card query - Asks the card for data (may return null)
  5. Fallback - Returns system ROM or floating bus value

Extended ROM Bank Selection:
The BankSelect property tracks which slot (1-7) currently owns the $C800-$CFFF extended ROM space. Accessing a slot's ROM ($Cn00-$CnFF) activates that slot; accessing $CFFF disables all extended ROM.

Configuration Management:
The Slots class implements IConfigurable (via ISlots), enabling complete save/restore of the slot configuration. The metadata format includes:

  • Which cards are installed in which slots (by ID)
  • Each card's configuration metadata (hierarchical inclusion)
  • Current BankSelect state (optional)

This allows the entire peripheral configuration to be saved, shared, and restored, including disk images, serial port settings, and other card-specific configurations. Empty slots are omitted from the metadata to keep it concise.

Threading:
This class is not thread-safe. All methods must be called from the emulator worker thread. Card installation/removal during emulation may cause race conditions.

Constructors

Slots(ICardFactory, ISystemRomProvider, IFloatingBusProvider, ISystemStatusProvider)

Initializes a new instance of the Slots class with all slots empty.

public Slots(ICardFactory factory, ISystemRomProvider rom, IFloatingBusProvider floatingBus, ISystemStatusProvider status)

Parameters

factory ICardFactory

The card factory for creating card instances.

rom ISystemRomProvider

The system ROM provider for $C000-$CFFF range.

floatingBus IFloatingBusProvider

The floating bus provider for unresponsive addresses.

status ISystemStatusProvider

The system status provider for soft switch states.

Remarks

All seven expansion slots (1-7) are initialized with NullCard instances, representing empty slots. Slot 0 is also filled with a NullCard since it's reserved for system use and should never be accessed.

The constructor creates independent NullCard instances via Clone() to ensure each slot has its own instance, even though NullCards are stateless.

Exceptions

ArgumentNullException

Thrown if any parameter is null.

InvalidOperationException

Thrown if the card factory cannot create a NullCard for initialization.

Properties

BankSelect

Gets or sets the currently active slot for extended ROM at $C800-$CFFF.

public byte BankSelect { get; set; }

Property Value

byte

A value from 0-7 where:

  • 0 = No slot active (extended ROM disabled)
  • 1-7 = That slot's extended ROM is active at $C800-$CFFF

Remarks

This property implements the Apple IIe's extended ROM bank switching mechanism. The hardware allows only one slot's extended ROM to be visible at $C800-$CFFF at any given time. The active slot is determined by:

  1. Reading/writing $Cn00-$CnFF sets BankSelect to slot n
  2. Reading/writing $CFFF sets BankSelect to 0 (disabled)

The bank selection state is preserved even when INTCXROM is enabled (which overrides extended ROM with system ROM but doesn't clear the selection). This allows the previously selected card's extended ROM to reappear when INTCXROM is disabled.

Example: A Disk II controller in slot 6 has boot ROM at $C600-$C6FF and extended driver code at its extended ROM. When the system reads $C600, slot 6's extended ROM becomes active at $C800-$CFFF. Reading $CFFF disables extended ROM.

this[ushort]

Gets or sets a byte at the specified address using array-style indexing.

public byte this[ushort address] { get; set; }

Parameters

address ushort

The address to access, offset by $C000. For example, to access $C600, use 0x0600.

Property Value

byte

The byte value at the specified address (reading or writing).

Examples

// Read from $C600
byte bootByte = slots[0x0600];

// Write to $C0EC (slot 6, I/O offset $0C)
slots[0x00EC] = 0x00;

Remarks

This indexer provides convenient array-style access to the slots address space, delegating to Read(ushort) for gets and Write(ushort, byte) for sets.

Size

Gets the size of the address space managed by this slots system.

public int Size { get; }

Property Value

int

Always returns 0x1000 (4096 bytes), representing the $C000-$CFFF range.

Remarks

Although the value indicates $C000-$CFFF (4KB), the slots system only handles $C090-$CFFF (3952 bytes). The range $C000-$C08F contains soft switches and other system I/O that's handled elsewhere (typically by VA2MBus).

If Read(ushort) or Write(ushort, byte) is called with an address in the $C000-$C08F range (0x0000-0x008F when offset), an InvalidOperationException will be thrown.

Methods

ApplyMetadata(string)

Applies slot configuration from JSON metadata, restoring installed cards and their configurations.

public bool ApplyMetadata(string metadata)

Parameters

metadata string

A JSON metadata string previously obtained from GetMetadata(), or an empty string to clear all slots.

Returns

bool

true if the configuration was successfully applied; false if the metadata was invalid or any card failed to apply its configuration.

Examples

// Save current configuration
string backup = slots.GetMetadata();

// Load a different configuration
if (!slots.ApplyMetadata(savedConfiguration))
{
    // Restore backup on failure
    slots.ApplyMetadata(backup);
}

// Clear all slots
slots.ApplyMetadata(string.Empty);

Remarks

This method restores the slot configuration from metadata by:

  1. Clearing all slots (removing all cards)
  2. Parsing the JSON metadata
  3. Installing each card by ID in its designated slot
  4. Recursively applying each card's embedded metadata
  5. Restoring the BankSelect state

Empty String Handling:
Passing an empty or whitespace-only string clears all slots, leaving the system with empty slots (all NullCard instances). This represents the default/unconfigured state.

Error Handling:
If any card fails to install or configure, this method returns false. The slot system may be left in a partial state where some cards are installed and others are not. For transactional behavior, callers should capture the current metadata before applying new metadata to enable rollback.

Card Factory Dependency:
This method relies on GetCardWithId(int) to create card instances. If a card ID in the metadata is not registered in the factory, that slot will remain empty and the method will return false.

GetCardIn(SlotNumber)

Retrieves the card currently installed in the specified slot.

public ICard GetCardIn(SlotNumber slot)

Parameters

slot SlotNumber

The slot number (1-7) to query.

Returns

ICard

The ICard instance installed in the slot. Returns a GetNullCard() instance if the slot is empty.

Remarks

This method provides direct access to the card instance for inspection, debugging, or advanced operations. Normal emulator operation doesn't require calling this method since Read(ushort) and Write(ushort, byte) automatically route to the appropriate card based on address.

Usage Examples: 

// Check what's installed in slot 6
ICard card = slots.GetCardIn(SlotNumber.Slot6);
Console.WriteLine($"Slot 6: {card.Name}");

// Verify a slot is empty
if (slots.GetCardIn(SlotNumber.Slot3).Id == 0) // NullCard.Id == 0
{
    Console.WriteLine("Slot 3 is empty");
}

GetMetadata()

Gets the current slot configuration as JSON metadata.

public string GetMetadata()

Returns

string

A JSON string containing the configuration of all installed cards and their metadata, or an empty string if serialization fails.

Remarks

This method captures the current state of all expansion slots, including which cards are installed and their individual configurations. The metadata uses JSON format with the following structure: 

{
  "version": 1,
  "bankSelect": 6,
  "slots": [
    {
      "slotNumber": 6,
      "cardId": 1,
      "cardName": "Disk II Controller",
      "metadata": "{...card-specific configuration...}"
    }
  ]
}

Hierarchical Configuration:
The slots system uses the inclusive approach to hierarchical configuration. Each installed card's metadata (obtained via GetMetadata()) is embedded within the slot configuration. This keeps the entire peripheral configuration self-contained and portable.

Empty Slots:
Empty slots (containing NullCard) are omitted from the metadata to keep it concise. On restoration, any slot not mentioned in the metadata is left empty.

BankSelect State:
The current BankSelect value is included in the metadata. While not strictly necessary for configuration (it's runtime state), including it allows for precise state restoration during debugging or save states.

InstallCard(int, SlotNumber)

Installs a peripheral card in the specified slot using its numeric card ID.

public void InstallCard(int id, SlotNumber slot)

Parameters

id int

The unique numeric identifier of the card type to install (see Id).

slot SlotNumber

The slot number (1-7) where the card should be installed.

Remarks

This method uses the ICardFactory to create a new instance of the specified card type via GetCardWithId(int). The card is cloned from a registered prototype, ensuring each slot gets an independent instance.

If a card is already installed in the specified slot, it is replaced by the new card. No state from the previous card is preserved.

Common card IDs:

  • 0 = NullCard (empty slot)
  • 1 = Disk II Controller
  • 2 = Super Serial Card

Exceptions

InvalidOperationException

Thrown if the card factory cannot create a card with the specified ID.

See Also
InstallCard(string, SlotNumber)
RemoveCard(SlotNumber)

InstallCard(string, SlotNumber)

Installs a peripheral card in the specified slot using its human-readable name.

public void InstallCard(string name, SlotNumber slot)

Parameters

name string

The name of the card type to install (see Name).

slot SlotNumber

The slot number (1-7) where the card should be installed.

Remarks

This method provides a more user-friendly alternative to InstallCard(int, SlotNumber) by accepting the card's display name instead of its numeric ID. The card name must exactly match the value returned by Name (case-sensitive).

Example usage: 

slots.InstallCard("Disk II Controller", SlotNumber.Slot6);
slots.InstallCard("Super Serial Card", SlotNumber.Slot2);

If a card is already installed in the specified slot, it is replaced by the new card.

Exceptions

InvalidOperationException

Thrown if the card factory cannot create a card with the specified name.

See Also
InstallCard(int, SlotNumber)
RemoveCard(SlotNumber)

IsEmpty(SlotNumber)

Determines whether the specified slot is empty (contains a NullCard).

public bool IsEmpty(SlotNumber slot)

Parameters

slot SlotNumber

The slot number (1-7) to check.

Returns

bool

true if the slot contains a GetNullCard() (empty slot); false if the slot contains an actual peripheral card.

Remarks

This is a convenience method that checks whether the card in the specified slot has an Id of 0 (the NullCard identifier). It provides a more readable alternative to manually checking the card ID.

Usage Examples: 

// Clear alternative to checking card ID
if (slots.IsEmpty(SlotNumber.Slot6))
{
    slots.InstallCard("Disk II Controller", SlotNumber.Slot6);
}

// List all installed cards
for (int i = 1; i <= 7; i++)
{
    var slot = (SlotNumber)(i - 1);
    if (!slots.IsEmpty(slot))
    {
        Console.WriteLine($"Slot {i}: {slots.GetCardIn(slot).Name}");
    }
}

This method is equivalent to: 

bool IsEmpty(SlotNumber slot) => GetCardIn(slot).Id == 0;
See Also
GetCardIn(SlotNumber)
RemoveCard(SlotNumber)

Read(ushort)

Reads a byte from the slots address space ($C090-$CFFF).

public byte Read(ushort address)

Parameters

address ushort

The address to read, offset by $C000. For example, to read from $C600, pass 0x0600. Valid range: 0x0090-0x0FFF.

Returns

byte

The byte value from the responding device (card, system ROM, or floating bus).

Remarks

Address Decoding Logic:

$C090-$C0FF (Card I/O Space):
If INTCXROM is enabled, returns system ROM. Otherwise, extracts slot number from address bits 4-6 and calls the card's ReadIO(byte). Returns floating bus if card returns null. This range never modifies BankSelect.

$C100-$C7FF (Card ROM Space):
If INTCXROM is enabled, returns system ROM. Otherwise, checks SLOTCXROM (or SLOTC3ROM for slot 3) to determine card vs. system ROM. If using card ROM, sets BankSelect to the slot number and calls ReadRom(byte). Returns floating bus if card returns null, or system ROM if soft switches indicate system ROM.

$C800-$CFFF (Extended ROM Space):
If INTCXROM is enabled, returns system ROM. Otherwise, checks BankSelect to determine which card's extended ROM is active. If BankSelect is non-zero, calls that card's ReadExtendedRom(ushort). Returns floating bus if BankSelect is 0 or if card returns null.

Special Behavior at $CFFF:
Reading from $CFFF (0x0FFF) disables extended ROM by setting BankSelect to 0. The read still returns the byte from the currently active card (if any) before being disabled.

Exceptions

InvalidOperationException

Thrown if the address is outside the valid range ($C090-$CFFF / 0x0090-0x0FFF).

See Also
Write(ushort, byte)
BankSelect

RemoveCard(SlotNumber)

Removes the card from the specified slot, replacing it with an empty slot (NullCard).

public void RemoveCard(SlotNumber slot)

Parameters

slot SlotNumber

The slot number (1-7) to clear.

Remarks

This method replaces the card in the specified slot with a GetNullCard() instance. The NullCard returns null for all read operations and no-ops for all writes, causing the slot to behave as if it's empty (returning floating bus values for reads).

Any state maintained by the removed card is lost. If the removed card was the active extended ROM slot (see BankSelect), the extended ROM remains active but will return floating bus values since NullCard returns null.

Note: This method cannot fail due to the card being "in use" since the emulator doesn't maintain references to installed cards outside the slots array.

Exceptions

InvalidOperationException

Thrown if the card factory cannot create a NullCard replacement.

See Also
InstallCard(int, SlotNumber)
InstallCard(string, SlotNumber)

Write(ushort, byte)

Writes a byte to the slots address space ($C090-$CFFF).

public void Write(ushort address, byte val)

Parameters

address ushort

The address to write, offset by $C000. For example, to write to $C600, pass 0x0600. Valid range: 0x0090-0x0FFF.

val byte

The byte value to write.

Remarks

Address Decoding Logic:

$C090-$C0FF (Card I/O Space):
If INTCXROM is enabled, writes to system ROM (usually a no-op). Otherwise, extracts slot number from address bits 4-6 and calls the card's WriteIO(byte, byte). Cards typically use this space for control registers, command triggers, and data output. This range never modifies BankSelect.

$C100-$C7FF (Card ROM Space):
If INTCXROM is enabled, writes to system ROM (usually a no-op). Otherwise, checks SLOTCXROM (or SLOTC3ROM for slot 3) to determine card vs. system ROM. If using card ROM, sets BankSelect to the slot number and calls WriteRom(byte, byte). Most cards treat this as a no-op since ROM is read-only, but some cards may implement writeable RAM in this space.

$C800-$CFFF (Extended ROM Space):
If INTCXROM is enabled, writes to system ROM (usually a no-op). Otherwise, if BankSelect is non-zero, calls that card's WriteExtendedRom(ushort, byte). If BankSelect is 0, the write is silently ignored (no floating bus effect).

Special Behavior at $CFFF:
Writing to $CFFF (0x0FFF) disables extended ROM by setting BankSelect to 0. The write still reaches the currently active card (if any) before being disabled.

Exceptions

InvalidOperationException

Thrown if the address is outside the valid range ($C090-$CFFF / 0x0090-0x0FFF).

See Also
Read(ushort)
BankSelect
Edit this page
Made with docfx
